data.title: Cognyte secures $20M support deal with EMEA National Security Agency
data.description:
• Cognyte Software (NASDAQ:CGNT) signed a one-year, $20M support agreement with a tier-1 national security agency in the EMEA region.
  • The customer has used Cognyte’s investigative analytics solutions for over a decade.
  • Cognyte’s technology helps the agency respond to complex threats including terrorism, organized crime, and trafficking.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb ends in red for seventh straight session
data.description:
Bristol-Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) extended losses for the seventh consecutive session on Wednesday as the stock ended 1.58% lower at $44.20.

Between September 16 and September 23, BMY lost over 3%. On a year-to-date basis, it has declined by nearly 21% compared to a 12.86% rise in the broader markets.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, it is recommended to Hold BMY. It has a score of 3.17 out of 5. The company has been graded A+ for profitability but holds a D rating for revisions. Wall Street analysts share the consensus rating of Hold.

However, Wall Street analysts were bullish on the stock, recommending a Buy rating. Overall, 13 analysts gave the company a Buy rating and 5 recommended to Hold the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Gen Alpha issued a Strong Buy call for the stock, calling BMY a “deep value opportunity”.

The analyst was optimistic about BMY driven by its next-generation portfolio fueling double-digit growth, as blockbuster drugs such as Reblozyl and Camzyos help counter declines in older products.

BMY’s strategic collaborations, strong pipeline, and growing presence in oncology and Alzheimer’s support its long-term relevance, according to the analyst.

“Bristol Myers Squibb stands out as a compelling deep value play in today’s volatile market. It offers a combination of income and strong upside potential from the current valuation,” Gen Alpha said in its article.

“BMY is backed by a strong balance sheet, an A credit rating, and a secure 5.5% dividend yield,” they added.

data.title: Immuneering announces public offering, $25M private placement with Sanofi; shares up 55%
data.description:
• Immuneering (NASDAQ:IMRX) has launched a proposed underwritten public offering of Class A common stock or pre-funded warrants.
  • The company may grant underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 15% additional shares or warrants.
  • Sanofi has agreed to invest $25M via a concurrent private placement at the public offering price.
  • Sanofi (SNY) may purchase Class A or non-voting Class B common stock in the private deal.
  • Both the offering and private placement are contingent on market conditions and the completion of the offering.
  • Proceeds will fund clinical and preclinical development of Immuneering’s oncology pipeline, as well as general corporate needs.
  • IMRX shares up 54.7% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Intel looks to Apple as Tan seeks to bring shine back to chipmaker: report
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) has allegedly approached Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to consider investing in the U.S. chip company following several colossal investments CEO Lip-Bu Tan recently secured for his company, according to Bloomberg.

Apple and Intel have discussed fostering a closer relationship as well, the report said, citing people familiar with the conversations. However, the talks are reportedly in a very early stage and may not materialize into action.

Intel shares jumped 6.4% by the close of market trading on Wednesday.

Tan has helped Intel secure a flurry of massive investments recently. This includes a $2B investment by Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF), a 10% stake by the U.S. government and, most recently, GPU giant Nvidia's (NVDA) $5B investment.

Apple, at one time, was a major customer for Intel. Apple began using Intel microprocessors in its Mac computers back in 2006. 

"Our goal is to provide our customers with the best personal computers in the world, and looking ahead Intel has the strongest processor roadmap by far," said Steve Jobs, Apple's CEO at the time.

However, in 2020, current Apple CEO Tim Cook announced plans to transition from Intel's x86 architecture to Apple silicon based on ARM (ARM) architecture. By 2023, Apple had completed the transition away from Intel-based Macs.

data.title: Nasdaq reports rise in short interest across listed securities as of September 15 settlement date
data.description:
Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) said on Wednesday that short interest across its listed securities increased as of the mid-September settlement date from August 29.

Short interest in 5,014 securities totaled 17,146,159,646 shares at the September 15 settlement date, compared to 4,988 issues and 16,958,657,192 shares at the end of August.

This is 2.01 days average daily volume, compared with an average of 1.97 days for the prior reporting period.

The open short interest positions reported for each Nasdaq security reflect the total number of shares sold short by all broker/dealers regardless of their exchange affiliations.

Short interest in 3,341 Nasdaq Global Market securities totaled 14,035,983,054 shares as of September 15.

Meanwhile, short interest in 1,673 securities on The Nasdaq Capital Market totaled 3,110,176,592 shares at the end of the September 15 settlement date.

data.title: KalVista to offer $110M in convertible notes; shares down 10%
data.description:
• KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) plans to offer $110M in convertible senior notes due 2031 via private placement.
  • An additional $15M in notes may be issued if initial purchasers exercise their option within 13 days.
  • The unsecured notes will mature on October 1, 2031, with semi-annual interest payments.
  • Notes are convertible under certain conditions and may be settled in stock, cash, or both, at KalVista’s discretion.
  • Redemption by KalVista is not allowed before Oct 1, 2028; partial redemption may occur after that if stock meets certain price conditions.
  • Holders can require repurchase at 100% of principal plus interest upon a "fundamental change."
  • Net proceeds will support working capital, EKTERLY commercialization, and potential future acquisitions, though no acquisitions are currently planned.
  • KALV shares down 10% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: KB Home GAAP EPS of $1.61 beats by $0.10, revenue of $1.62B beats by $30M
data.description:
• KB Home press release (NYSE:KBH): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.61 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $1.62B (-7.4% Y/Y) beats by $30M.

  • The company is providing the following guidance for its 2025 full year:

  • Housing revenues in the range of $6.10 billion to $6.20 billion.
  • Average selling price of approximately $483,000.
  • Homebuilding operating income as a percentage of revenues of approximately 8.9%, assuming no inventory-related charges.
  • Housing gross profit margin in the range of 19.2% to 19.3%, assuming no inventory-related charges.
  • Selling, general and administrative expenses as a percentage of housing revenues in the range of 10.2% to 10.3%.
  • Effective tax rate of approximately 23%.
  • Ending community count of approximately 260.
  • Shares -0.45% AH.

data.title: Lithium Americas surges 95% on report of possible U.S. government stake but 'clock is ticking'
data.description:
Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC) shares nearly doubled Wednesday, settling +95.7%, following reports that the Trump administration is seeking an equity stake in the company as it renegotiates terms of a $2.3 billion U.S. government loan.

The company acknowledged it is in talks with the Department of Energy and General Motors, its joint venture partner in the Thacker Pass lithium project in Nevada, regarding the conditions of its loan but did not comment on the reports of a potential government stake.

The news also lifted other lithium miners, including Standard Lithium (SLI) +20.1%, Lithium Argentina (LAR) +7.5%, Sigma Lithium (SGML) +7.4%, Albemarle (ALB) +1.8%, SQM (SQM) +1.1%.

The clock is ticking for Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC), which is experiencing a cash crunch and needs the government funds to keep operating, according to The Wall Street Journal, which added that if the company is unable to reach a deal with the Trump administration, it may be forced to mothball the Thacker Pass project.

U.S. officials reportedly are concerned about the viability of the project and wary that funds would be invested in a volatile industry dominated by Chinese players.

A government stake "would lend credence to project completion and expansion to multiple phases with perhaps enhanced economics," TD Cowen analyst David Deckelbaum said in a note, and media reports are "provocative considering outsized returns in names like Intel and MP, but tangibly for LAC, the upside is less clear given committed offtake constraints, though certainly improves confidence in long-term financing."

To the extent the U.S. government believes equity stakes have a significantly lower political cost than tax increases, "mark-to-market equity stakes can offset deficit financing, higher deficit spending can support corporate profits, interest rates can trend lower to support equity valuations, and equity markets can view equity stakes as a leading indicator" of favorable return on invested capital, Jefferies analysts wrote.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:LIT), (ILIT), (BATT)

data.title: Steelcase Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.09, revenue of $897.1M beats by $23.5M
data.description:
• Steelcase press release (NYSE:SCS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $897.1M (+4.8% Y/Y) beats by $23.5M.

  • Other income, net of $3.0 million in the second quarter represented an improvement of $3.6 million compared to other expense, net in the prior year, primarily due to higher income from unconsolidated affiliates.
